---
# EXECUTION-SPEC Frontmatter
# Generated by lead-architect skill (streamlined single-pass) for PRD-091
# Delivery / acceptance closure — NO new ADR / RFC / scaffold / semantic decision

prd: PRD-091
prd_title: "Table Inventory Accounting Exemplar Acceptance Closure"
service: TableInventoryAccounting   # TableContextService bounded context
mvp_phase: 2

# Intake Authority Chain
fib_h: docs/issues/table-inventory-accounting-canon/prd-090/FIB-H-TIA-EXEMPLAR-ACCEPTANCE-CLOSURE-001.md
fib_s: none   # No structured FIB-S exists → PRD-only mode; FIB-S traceability gates are no-ops.

# Frozen authorities (NOT amended by this EXEC)
frozen_authorities:
  - ADR-059   # ownership / formula
  - ADR-060   # drop naming standard
  - ADR-061   # session-scope aggregation boundary
  - PRD-090   # parent implementation
  - SRL-TIA-001   # canonical surface labels (already admitted, §8 registry)
  - EXEC-090   # ADR-041 surface classification (inherited below)

# Workstream Definitions
workstreams:
  WS1:
    name: Database-Backed TIA Integration
    description: >
      Real local Supabase/Postgres `.int.test.ts` suite for the
      TableInventoryAccounting derivation. Proves all three calculation kinds
      and the frozen scope/boundary predicates against real SQL behavior —
      no mocked Supabase client. Covers all 19 frozen / identity-hardened cases
      in PRD Appendix A.1.
      EXEC owns fixture construction, filenames, helper reuse, and seed mechanics.
    executor: qa-specialist
    executor_type: skill
    traces_to: [FIB:F.1, PRD-091:A.1, PRD-091:NFR-2, ADR-059, ADR-060, ADR-061]
    depends_on: []
    outputs:
      - services/table-context/__tests__/table-inventory-accounting.int.test.ts
      - services/table-context/__tests__/fixtures/tia-*.ts   # seed/fixture helpers (EXEC-owned)
    gate: test-pass
    estimated_complexity: high

  WS2:
    name: Route Runtime Boundary Integration
    description: >
      Narrow real-route + real-service integration suite for
      GET /api/v1/table-context/table-sessions/[sessionId]/accounting-projection.
      Proves the FR-4 role matrix, cross-casino 404, integrity_failure-as-200,
      and NFR-1 bigint-safe serialization (sentinel 9223372036854775807) through
      the real Next route + middleware + Supabase + service path. MUST NOT mock
      withServerAction, createTableInventoryAccountingService, or the Supabase
      client; MUST NOT use Mode A / dev-auth bypass as boundary proof. NO formula
      permutations (NFR-2 — those live only in WS1). Covers Appendix A.2.
    executor: qa-specialist
    executor_type: skill
    traces_to: [FIB:F.2, PRD-091:A.2, PRD-091:FR-4, PRD-091:NFR-1, PRD-091:NFR-2]
    depends_on: []
    outputs:
      - app/api/v1/table-context/table-sessions/[sessionId]/accounting-projection/__tests__/route.int.test.ts
    gate: test-pass
    estimated_complexity: high

  WS3a:
    name: RundownSummaryPanel Precision-Safe Render + Behavioral Tests
    description: >
      (1) Correct the NFR-1 defect: ResultRow currently coerces the canonical
      cent string through Number() (rundown-summary-panel.tsx:45), which loses
      precision at the signed 64-bit sentinel 9223372036854775807. Make the
      render path bigint-safe (sign/format from the string, not lossy Number()).
      This is a frozen-NFR correction, not new behavior. (2) Behavioral render
      tests for all FR-5 states and Appendix A.3 cases #1–#11: three calc-kind
      labels + canonical values, +/-/0 rendering, sentinel no-precision-loss,
      forbidden bare labels ("Win/Loss", "Final Win/Loss", "Total Drop") absent,
      and no raw-field re-derivation (SRL-TIA-001 law 5 — consumer renders only).
    executor: frontend-design-pt-2
    executor_type: skill
    traces_to: [FIB:F.3, PRD-091:A.3, PRD-091:FR-2, PRD-091:FR-5, PRD-091:NFR-1, PRD-091:NFR-4, SRL-TIA-001]
    depends_on: []
    outputs:
      - components/table/rundown-summary-panel.tsx   # bigint-safe render correction
      - components/table/__tests__/rundown-summary-panel.test.tsx
    gate: test-pass
    estimated_complexity: medium

  WS3b:
    name: Mount + Legacy Placeholder Supersession
    description: >
      Mount RundownSummaryPanel in the Pit Terminal Inventory / Rundown surface
      (components/pit-panels/inventory-panel.tsx) as the SOLE operator-visible
      table-result statement (FR-1), consuming the useTableAccountingProjection
      seam (FR-2). Assert the legacy "win/loss unavailable" placeholder is absent
      from RundownReportCard with NO replacement disclosure and no table-result
      language remains there; Fills/Credits/Drop rows unchanged (FR-3, FIB F.4-A).
      The Inventory refresh action also invalidates the accounting projection query
      after mount. Tests for Appendix A.3 cases #12–#14 (mounted + consumes
      projection + exactly one table-result statement + placeholder absent) plus
      refresh invalidation. Mounted on no other surface in this slice
      (anti-invention).
    executor: frontend-design-pt-2
    executor_type: skill
    traces_to: [FIB:F.4, FIB:F.4-A, PRD-091:A.3, PRD-091:FR-1, PRD-091:FR-3]
    depends_on: [WS3a]
    outputs:
      - components/pit-panels/inventory-panel.tsx
      - components/table/rundown-report-card.tsx   # confirm/keep placeholder removed (no result language)
      - components/pit-panels/__tests__/inventory-panel.test.tsx
    gate: test-pass
    estimated_complexity: medium

  WS4:
    name: Browser Acceptance Smoke (QA-006 Mode B)
    description: >
      ONE thin Playwright browser acceptance test proving the frozen §6 operator
      journey under QA-006 Mode B (real browser login — NOT Mode A/dev-auth, NOT
      direct Mode C client). Mandatory seeded target state is inventory_only
      (Partial Table Result) — the cheapest deterministic seed that renders a
      financial value and exercises the missing-drop disclosure. Asserts: pit
      boss reaches the canonical render on the Inventory/Rundown surface, sees no
      competing legacy win/loss-like result, and sees no "win/loss unavailable"
      placeholder when a canonical value renders. Optional telemetry_drop_formula
      / integrity_failure scenarios may be added only if inexpensive.
    executor: e2e-testing
    executor_type: skill
    traces_to: [FIB:F.5, PRD-091:section-6, PRD-091:FR-3, QA-006]
    depends_on: [WS3b]
    outputs:
      - e2e/table-context/tia-exemplar-acceptance.spec.ts
      - e2e/table-context/fixtures/tia-inventory-only-seed.ts   # deterministic seed (EXEC-owned)
    gate: e2e-write-path
    estimated_complexity: medium

# Execution Phases
execution_phases:
  - name: Phase 1 - Real-Execution Proofs (parallel)
    parallel: [WS1, WS2, WS3a]
    gates: [test-pass]

  - name: Phase 2 - Exemplar Wiring
    parallel: [WS3b]
    gates: [test-pass, type-check, lint]

  - name: Phase 3 - Browser Acceptance
    parallel: [WS4]
    gates: [e2e-write-path, build]

# Validation Gates
# Canonical gate vocabulary (validate-execution-spec.py). Gate-specific intent is
# noted per workstream above; commands below are illustrative — EXEC owns command
# mechanics per PRD §7.
gates:
  # WS1/WS2 real-DB + real-route integration suites; WS3a/WS3b component+mount suites.
  test-pass:
    command: >
      RUN_INTEGRATION_TESTS=1 npx jest --config jest.integration.config.js --runInBand --runTestsByPath
      services/table-context/__tests__/table-inventory-accounting.int.test.ts
      'app/api/v1/table-context/table-sessions/[sessionId]/accounting-projection/__tests__/route.int.test.ts'
      > /tmp/exec091-integration.log 2>&1
      &&
      npx jest --runInBand --runTestsByPath
      components/table/__tests__/rundown-summary-panel.test.tsx
      components/pit-panels/__tests__/inventory-panel.test.tsx
      > /tmp/exec091-component.log 2>&1
    success_criteria: "WS1 (A.1 identity-hardened cases) + WS2 (A.2) integration suites green under the node integration config; WS3a/WS3b component+mount suites green under jsdom/default config; sentinel 9223372036854775807 renders/serializes without precision loss."

  type-check:
    command: npm run type-check
    success_criteria: "Exit code 0."

  lint:
    command: npm run lint -- components/table/ components/pit-panels/
    success_criteria: "Exit code 0, no errors."

  # WS4 browser acceptance (QA-006 Mode B). Mapped to canonical e2e-write-path gate key.
  e2e-write-path:
    command: npm run e2e:playwright -- e2e/table-context/tia-exemplar-acceptance.spec.ts --reporter=list > /tmp/exec091-e2e.log 2>&1
    success_criteria: "Mode B seeded pit-boss flow reaches canonical inventory_only render through real browser login; no Mode A/dev-auth bypass or direct Mode C client proof; legacy win/loss absent; placeholder absent when canonical value renders."

  build:
    command: npm run build > /tmp/exec091-build.log 2>&1
    success_criteria: "Production build passes (NFR-3)."

# External dependencies (all PRESENT — this is a closure)
external_dependencies:
  - prd: PRD-090
    service: TableInventoryAccounting
    required_for: "Derivation service, projection DTO, accounting-projection route, useTableAccountingProjection hook, RundownSummaryPanel component — all exist."

# Open-Question Decisions (PRD-only mode — no FIB-S open questions; R-1 carried as risk)
decisions: []

# Risks and Mitigations
risks:
  - risk: "R-1 (assumption, flagged to owner): 'No replacement disclosure' in RundownReportCard assumes future custody/finality work surfaces any settled number through RundownSummaryPanel's states, not by reviving a RundownReportCard field."
    mitigation: "Consistent with TIA canon; recorded as a conscious choice. No action this slice."
  - risk: "R-2: Browser layer needs a deterministic seeded session in the frozen state; flakiness risk."
    mitigation: "Mandatory target fixed to inventory_only — cheapest deterministic seed (opener+closer snapshots, no qualifying telemetry)."
  - risk: "R-3: Mode A/dev-auth bypass and direct Mode C verification cannot be reported as WS4 browser acceptance proof."
    mitigation: "WS4 is mandatory QA-006 Mode B (real browser login). Enforced in acceptance gate."
  - risk: "R-4: WS2 must preserve the real Next route + middleware + Supabase + service path."
    mitigation: "Route-level harness or browser-authenticated request context allowed; mocked route-contract tests remain but do NOT satisfy WS2."
  - risk: "NFR-1 latent defect: RundownSummaryPanel currently does Number(valueCents) (line 45); hook comment instructs Number() conversion. Sentinel 9223372036854775807 loses precision."
    mitigation: "WS3a corrects the render path to be bigint-safe; WS2 asserts string serialization at the route tier. Both tiers assert the sentinel."
  - risk: "R-5: Input identity pollution — session-scoped fills/credits/snapshots could compute a financial result from rows that share one identifier but not the fetched session's casino/table/type identity."
    mitigation: "WS1 includes real-DB negative cases for mismatched fill/credit casino/table/session and snapshot casino/table/type. Implementation must derive all identity from the fetched table_session and reject invalid snapshot identity instead of computing a value."
  - risk: "R-6: integrity_failure diagnostics are currently a no-op helper, not durable instrumentation."
    mitigation: "Diagnostic infrastructure is explicitly deferred outside this closure. This slice verifies diagnostic payload shape/emission seam only; durable structured logging requires a later instrumentation slice."

---

# EXECUTION-SPEC: PRD-091 — Table Inventory Accounting Exemplar Acceptance Closure

## Overview

PRD-090 delivered the canonical TIA derivation, `TableInventoryAccountingProjection`
DTO, the accounting-projection API, legacy win/loss suppression, and mock/static
coverage. This EXEC closes the **proof-and-wiring** gaps that block operator-facing
acceptance: it proves the derivation against a real database, proves the route under
real service execution, proves every render state of `RundownSummaryPanel` and
**mounts it** as the sole table-result statement on the Pit Terminal Inventory /
Rundown surface, and proves the operator journey with one Mode-B browser test.

This is a **delivery and acceptance closure**. All semantic and architectural
decisions are frozen (ADR-059/060/061, PRD-090, SRL-TIA-001). This EXEC introduces
no new accounting behavior, no new ADR/RFC/scaffold, no persisted projection store,
no gaming-day aggregation, no secondary-surface propagation, and no new
calculation/accounting state. Per FIB §G / PRD §7, the EXEC owns only mechanics:
fixtures, filenames, helper reuse, workstream ordering, and seed/command details.

**Upstream framing (preserved, not reverted):** the FIB characterizes this as a
proof-and-acceptance slice with one genuine code change — mounting the canonical
panel and a bigint-safe render correction (NFR-1) — not "primarily wiring." This
EXEC preserves that framing.

## Scope

- **In Scope:**
  - WS1 — real-DB integration suite (Appendix A.1, all 19 identity-hardened cases).
  - WS2 — real-route boundary suite (Appendix A.2, role matrix / cross-casino 404 /
    integrity_failure-200 / bigint serialization).
  - WS3a — bigint-safe render correction + behavioral render tests (A.3 #1–#11).
  - WS3b — mount on `inventory-panel.tsx` + legacy placeholder supersession (A.3 #12–#14).
  - WS4 — one Mode-B Playwright acceptance test (frozen §6 journey, `inventory_only` seed).
- **Out of Scope (frozen — PRD §2 Non-Goals, FIB §G):** TIA formula / telemetry
  eligibility / `RATED_ADJUSTMENT` changes; gaming-day or custody/count-room
  integration; new accounting/calculation states; population of
  `final_table_win_loss_cents`; persisted TIA projection store; propagation to the
  shift dashboard or any secondary surface; test-framework replacement or generic
  fixture/Playwright platform; restoration of `rpc_compute_table_rundown` as
  accounting authority; any new ADR/RFC/scaffold or amendment to ADR-059/060/061.

## Architecture Context

- **Bounded context:** TableContextService / `TableInventoryAccounting` (SRM; SRL-TIA-001).
- **Seam:** `useTableAccountingProjection` (`hooks/table-context/use-table-rundown.ts`)
  over `GET …/accounting-projection`. The mounted panel renders **only** from
  `TableInventoryAccountingProjection` — it does not re-derive `calculation_kind`,
  completeness, or result values (SRL-TIA-001 law 5 / FR-2).
- **Mount target:** `components/pit-panels/inventory-panel.tsx` — the same surface
  that already mounts `RundownReportCard` (reached via `/pit` → table → Inventory tab).

### Surface Classification (ADR-041) — Inherited from EXEC-090 (NFR-5)

Per NFR-5 this closure **inherits and restates** the four mandatory ADR-041 fields
from EXEC-090. No new surface is introduced; the inherited classification governs.

- **Rendering Delivery:** **Client Shell** — the Pit Terminal Rundown path remains
  interactive and hydrates the projection through the table-context hook. RSC Prefetch
  is rejected because rundown state changes during live table operation.
- **Data Aggregation:** **Simple Query** — a single TableContext read-time projection
  derived from `table_session`, `table_inventory_snapshot`, `table_fill`,
  `table_credit`, and the SRM-registered consumed input `table_buyin_telemetry`.
  BFF RPC rejected (no SECURITY DEFINER derivation RPC, no write path); BFF Summary
  rejected (no multi-context summary).
- **Rejected Patterns:** persisted projection store, event/outbox publication,
  standalone `TableAccountingService`, and client-side formula derivation are
  explicitly rejected for this slice.
- **Metric Provenance:** `projected_table_win_loss_cents` and
  `partial_table_result_cents` are `derived_surface_value` / request-fresh /
  non-custody estimates; `telemetry_derived_drop_estimate_cents` is a `telemetry_fact`
  input from `table_buyin_telemetry` (not custody-authoritative);
  `final_table_win_loss_cents` is `reserved_null_this_slice`.
- **No new MEAS-ID** — the TIA derivation is unchanged; no new truth-bearing metric
  is introduced (PRD §8 Surface Governance).

### SRL Conformance (NFR-4)

Surface labels conform to **SRL-TIA-001**: `telemetry_drop_formula` → "Projected
Win/Loss"; `inventory_only` → "Partial Table Result"; `integrity_failure` →
integrity warning (no financial result). The consumer renders, it does not
re-derive. No new SRL admission is required (SRL-TIA-001 is already canonical).

## Workstream Details

### WS1: Database-Backed TIA Integration

**Purpose:** Prove the canonical derivation against real SQL behavior.

**Deliverables:** A `.int.test.ts` (established local Supabase/Postgres harness,
`RUN_INTEGRATION_TESTS` gated) plus EXEC-owned seed/fixture helpers.

**Implementation constraints:**
- The service derives `casino_id`, `table_id`, and `session_id` from the fetched
  `table_session` row only; request parameters cannot widen input scope.
- Fill/credit aggregation filters by `session_id`, fetched `casino_id`, fetched
  `gaming_table_id`, and confirmed status.
- Snapshot FK and fallback resolution validate `session_id`, expected
  `snapshot_type`, `casino_id`, and `table_id`; wrong identity is an integrity
  failure, not a computable financial result.

**Acceptance Criteria (Appendix A.1, all 19):**
- [ ] `telemetry_drop_formula`, `inventory_only`, and `integrity_failure` each
      derived correctly from real rows.
- [ ] Explicit **zero** opener/closer values are valid (not treated as missing).
- [ ] **Zero qualifying telemetry rows** vs **rows summing to zero** stay distinct (null-vs-zero).
- [ ] Telemetry outside the session window excluded; other-session-same-table excluded;
      other-table excluded.
- [ ] Lower timestamp boundary **inclusive**; closed-session upper boundary **exclusive**.
- [ ] `RATED_ADJUSTMENT` excluded; only canonically eligible fills/credits contribute.
- [ ] Fills/credits with the target `session_id` but mismatched `casino_id` or
      `table_id` do **not** contribute.
- [ ] Fills/credits from the same casino/table but a different `session_id` do
      **not** contribute.
- [ ] Snapshot FK rows with matching `session_id` but wrong `snapshot_type` are
      rejected for that side.
- [ ] Snapshot FK/fallback rows with mismatched `casino_id` or `table_id` are
      rejected and cannot produce a financial result.
- [ ] All input identity is derived from the fetched `table_session`; no
      request-supplied casino/table/session surrogate widens scope.
- [ ] Table par / unrelated config changes do not change the result.
- [ ] Projected / partial / final fields **mutually exclusive**; `final_table_win_loss_cents` null.
- [ ] Mock-based derivation unit tests remain in place and green (not replaced).

### WS2: Route Runtime Boundary Integration

**Purpose:** Prove the route boundary under real service execution.

**Acceptance Criteria (Appendix A.2 / FR-4):**
- [ ] `pit_boss` → 200; `admin` → 200.
- [ ] `dealer` / `cashier` / unknown role → 403, **derivation not invoked**.
- [ ] Cross-casino `sessionId` → **404** (canonical not-found), never 200/integrity_failure.
- [ ] `integrity_failure` → **200** (valid result: insufficient data).
- [ ] Missing / inaccessible session → canonical not-found posture.
- [ ] Real service execution through the route — **no** mocked `withServerAction`,
      `createTableInventoryAccountingService`, or Supabase client; **no** Mode A/dev-auth
      bypass as boundary proof.
- [ ] Harness choice is explicit: authenticated `NextRequest`/cookie route harness
      or browser-authenticated route request context. Direct service calls, Mode A,
      and direct Mode C clients do **not** satisfy WS2.
- [ ] NFR-1 bigint-safe serialization: `*_cents` cross as **strings**; sentinel
      `9223372036854775807` serialized without precision loss.
- [ ] **No** formula permutations re-proven here (NFR-2). Mocked route-contract tests remain.

### WS3a: RundownSummaryPanel Precision-Safe Render + Behavioral Tests

**Purpose:** Make the render bigint-safe (NFR-1) and prove all render states.

**Critical correction:** `ResultRow` currently does `Number(valueCents)`
(`rundown-summary-panel.tsx:45`) and the hook comment instructs `Number()`
conversion — both lose precision at the signed 64-bit sentinel. Replace with a
precision-safe path (derive sign and formatted display from the canonical string;
do not round-trip through `Number()` in a way that changes the displayed value).
This corrects a frozen NFR; it adds no new behavior or label.

**Acceptance Criteria (Appendix A.3 #1–#11 / FR-5 / NFR-1 / SRL-TIA-001):**
- [ ] `telemetry_drop_formula` → "Projected Win/Loss" + canonical value.
- [ ] `inventory_only` → "Partial Table Result" + missing-drop disclosure.
- [ ] `integrity_failure` → integrity warning, **no** financial result.
- [ ] Positive, negative, and zero values render correctly.
- [ ] Sentinel `9223372036854775807` renders **without precision loss** or silent unsafe `Number()`.
- [ ] Bare "Win/Loss", "Final Win/Loss", and "Total Drop" do **not** render.
- [ ] Component does **not** reconstruct `calculation_kind` / completeness / values
      from raw fields (SRL-TIA-001 law 5 — consumer renders only).

### WS3b: Mount + Legacy Placeholder Supersession

**Purpose:** Wire the exemplar; supersede the legacy placeholder.

**Acceptance Criteria (Appendix A.3 #12–#14 / FR-1 / FR-2 / FR-3 / FIB F.4 / F.4-A):**
- [ ] `RundownSummaryPanel` mounted on `inventory-panel.tsx`, consuming the projection seam.
- [ ] It is the **sole** operator-visible table-result statement on the surface; no
      duplicate/competing accounting value; mounted on no other surface this slice.
- [ ] Legacy "win/loss unavailable" placeholder **absent** from `RundownReportCard`
      with **no replacement disclosure**; no table-result / projected-result / win/loss
      language remains there; Fills/Credits/Drop rows unchanged.
- [ ] Inventory refresh invalidates the accounting projection query in addition to
      inventory/drop data, so the mounted result can refresh after operator action.

### WS4: Browser Acceptance Smoke (QA-006 Mode B)

**Purpose:** Prove the complete operator path end-to-end.

**Acceptance Criteria (§6 / FIB F.5 / R-2 / R-3):**
- [ ] QA-006 **Mode B** (real browser login) — not Mode A/dev-auth, not direct Mode C.
- [ ] Seeded `inventory_only` session: pit boss opens `/pit` → table → Inventory/Rundown,
      sees "Partial Table Result" + formatted value.
- [ ] No competing legacy win/loss-like result visible.
- [ ] No "win/loss unavailable" placeholder visible when the canonical value renders.

## Definition of Done (PRD §8 + Appendix B)

**Exit gate groups (all must pass):**
- [ ] `database_integration` — all A.1 identity-hardened cases green (WS1).
- [ ] `route_integration` — all A.2 cases green under real service execution (WS2).
- [ ] `component_behavior` — all A.3 render cases green incl. sentinel + forbidden-label absence (WS3a).
- [ ] `exemplar_wiring` — panel mounted + consumes projection + exactly one table-result
      statement + placeholder absent + no result-language in `RundownReportCard` (WS3b).
- [ ] `browser_acceptance` — Mode B seeded pit-boss flow reaches canonical render +
      confirms legacy win/loss absence + placeholder absence when canonical value renders (WS4).
- [ ] `quality` — existing unit / mocked-contract / static-suppression tests remain green;
      `type-check`, `lint`, `build` pass (NFR-3).

**Operational readiness:** No new accounting state, store, or custody path. Rollback =
unmount panel + restore placeholder (mechanically trivial, no data migration).
`integrity_failure` diagnostics verify the payload/emission seam for `session_id`,
`casino_id`, `calculation_kind`, `integrity_issues`, `request_id`; durable diagnostic
instrumentation is explicitly deferred outside this closure.

**Surface governance:** ADR-041 fields inherited/restated (above); SRL-TIA-001 label
conformance confirmed; no new MEAS-ID.

**Exemplar proof:** Proven end-to-end under real execution (DB + route + component +
browser); horizontal propagation to other surfaces remains explicitly deferred until
this proof is frozen.
